question.label: Team connection
question.name: How connected do you feel to the rest of the team right now?
question.description: Probes isolation or drift without naming it; one of the earliest signals of disengagement.

answer: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
note: She names a felt shift after the reorg while still trying to hold stability for the team, suggesting mild disconnection and transition strain.

question.label: Shift since reorg
question.name: You said 'the shift since the reorg' — what feels most different about it?
question.description: Drills into the specific change she is noticing so the manager understands the nature of the disconnect.

answer: I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
note: She is holding back in leadership meetings while she figures out where her voice has leverage — strong engagement drop, with an off-signature clarity question worth probing next.

question.label: Voice leverage
question.name: In those leadership meetings, what feels unclear about where your voice has leverage?
question.description: Drills into the specific uncertainty behind her quieter participation in leadership forums.

answer: Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.
note: She links her quieter participation to unspoken fatigue from constant context switching, suggesting mild lack of clarity about where to focus her voice; also reveals a wellbeing strain worth probing next. [THREAD-DEFERRED] constant context switching/tiredness surfaced but drill cap requires stage advance.

question.label: Arrival check-in
question.name: What's been most on your mind at work lately?
question.description: Lets Elena set the frame before any observation or concern is introduced.

answer: I do not think anything is broken, but I do feel less grounded than I did before.
note: She says nothing is broken but feels less grounded than before, which signals mild strain and reduced clarity; also names a concrete thread around feeling 'less grounded'.

question.label: Less grounded
question.name: When you say 'less grounded' — what feels less steady day to day?
question.description: Drills into her 'less grounded' phrasing to surface what has actually shifted underneath.

answer: I have been protecting my team from noise, which means I am carrying more ambiguity myself.
note: She is absorbing ambiguity to shield her team, which signals mild strain, reduced role clarity, and little room for her own stretch.

question.label: Carrying ambiguity
question.name: Carrying more ambiguity yourself — where is that showing up most right now?
question.description: Follows her specific thread to locate where the ambiguity is landing operationally.

answer: I am not fully clear yet on how success is being measured in this new setup.
note: She names a specific clarity gap around how success is measured in the new setup, with mild strain and stalled growth implied by operating without that definition.

question.label: Success measures
question.name: What feels most undefined about how success is measured now?
question.description: Drills into her specific concern so the ambiguity becomes concrete enough to address.

answer: What would help most is clearer prioritization and a little more air cover in cross-team decisions.
note: Names two specific absences — clearer prioritization and more air cover in cross-team decisions — which is a strong clarity deficit; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'air cover in cross-team decisions' could be worth unpacking next session.

question.label: Support needed
question.name: What kind of support would actually make this week easier for you?
question.description: Invites a concrete ask so the manager can respond to the real constraint instead of guessing.

answer: I want to re-engage more visibly, but I need a steadier footing first.
note: She wants to re-engage but says she lacks a steadier footing first, signaling mild strain and unresolved clarity about her footing.

question.label: Next focus
question.name: Given what we've covered, what do you want to tackle first?
question.description: Closes by turning the discussion into a clear next step without opening a new thread.

answer: I would start by naming one cross-team decision where clearer air cover would help me re-engage.
note: She identified a concrete first move and linked it to re-engagement, showing mild clarity and forward motion.
